channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: WhatsApp vs. Apple Messages: Which is the better messaging app in 2024?
item.description: Messaging apps are one of the most frequently used apps on our phones. There’s probably not a day that goes by when most people don’t hear a ding alerting them to a new text. There’s also no shortage of messaging apps.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: The shocking rise and fall of elite cybersecurity firm IronNet
item.description: The future was once dazzling for IronNet. Founded by a former director of the National Security Agency and stacked with elite members of the U.S. intelligence establishment, IronNet promised it was going to revolutionize the way governments and corporations combat cyberattacks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Navigating the federal AI frontier: Top priorities for incoming chief AI officers
item.description: There are ten key focus areas for current and prospective agency CAIOs to consider to ensure and maximize the benefits and minimize the risks of AI. The post Navigating the federal AI frontier: Top priorities for incoming chief AI officers first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Civilization 7 makers work with Shawnee to bring sincere representation of the tribe to the game
item.description: MIAMI, Okla. (AP) — Shawnee Tribe Chief Ben Barnes grew up playing video games, including “probably hundreds of hours” colonizing a distant planet in the 1999 title Sid Meier's Alpha Centauri.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taxpayers in California and 23 other states will be able to file their returns directly with the IRS in 2025
item.description: The federal tax collector’s Direct File program, which allows taxpayers to calculate and submit their returns to the government directly without using commercial tax preparation software, will be open to more than 30 million people in 24 states.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Scammers are flooding Trump’s new crypto exchange
item.description: Back in September, Donald Trump announced his new crypto exchange, World Liberty Financial (WLFI). Now, registered investors can officially sign up for the whitelist—assuming they can avoid the scammers.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Open AI is testing a new interface for ChatGPT — ‘Canvas’
item.description: Open AI has unveiled Canvas, a new ChatGPT interface specifically crafted for developers that is now available in beta. Canvas has been developed using GPT-4o and makes it possible, among other things, to use a separate window for code.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft Visual Studio Code Editor Gets Major Update with Version 1.94
item.description: Microsoft releases major update to its Visual Studio Code editor, version 1.94, featuring improved file search and Python testing capabilities
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why OpenAI needs another $6.6 billion in VC money
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Google's search engine's latest AI injection will answer voiced questions about video and photos
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is injecting its search engine with more artificial intelligence that will enable people to voice questions about images and occasionally organize an entire page of results, despite the technology's past misadventures with misleading information.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Google CEO eyeing electricity from nuclear plants for its data centers
item.description: Google CEO Sundar Pichai is considering using electricity from nuclear plants to power his company’s data centers, as the tech giant contends with the expansive energy demands of artificial intelligence (AI).
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: How data brokers are fueling elder fraud in America
item.description: Criminals are increasingly turning to data brokers to provide access to private information that can be used to improve their scamming schemes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suncor Energy derives business value through business AI and automation
item.description: By the beginning of the decade, though, its automated solution was outmoded – a troubling predicament given Suncor’s legal and regulatory obligation to charge taxes on intercompany transactions.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Windows 11 Insider Previews: What’s in the latest build?
item.description: The Windows 11 2023 Update has been released, but behind the scenes, Microsoft is constantly working to improve the newest version of Windows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tres cosas que hay que hacer bien en la gestión de datos para proyectos de IA generativa
item.description: Según Kari Briski, vicepresidente de Modelos, Software y Servicios de IA en Nvidia, implementar con éxito la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa depende de la gestión eficaz de los datos y de evaluar cómo los diferentes modelos trabajan juntos para servir a un caso de uso específico.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Why OpenAI is becoming a for-profit business
item.description: OpenAI’s plans to restructure as a for-profit business mark a significant departure from its roots as a nonprofit research lab grounded in a commitment to build artificial intelligence (AI) to “benefit humanity.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: OpenAI updates API with model distillation, prompt caching abilities
item.description: In what can only be seen as OpenAI’s efforts to catch up with rivals, the ChatGPT-maker released several updates to its API to help ease the development of generative AI-based applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI on the mainframe? IBM may be onto something
item.description: CIOs have been moving workloads from legacy platforms to the cloud for more than a decade but the rush to AI may breathe new life into an old enterprise friend: the mainframe. At least IBM believes so.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Understanding VBS Enclaves, Windows’ new security technology
item.description: Recently Microsoft announced efforts to improve security in its planned Recall AI assistant. Many of the details were not a surprise, as it built on familiar tools and services I’ve written about during the past few years, but the most important feature, VBS Enclaves, will be new to most of us.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Hurricane Helene brings climate change to forefront of the presidential campaign
item.description: The devastation wrought by Hurricane Helene has brought climate change to the forefront of the presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: OpenAI previews Realtime API for speech-to-speech apps
item.description: OpenAI has introduced a public beta of the Realtime API, an API that allows paid developers to build low-latency, multi-modal experiences including text and speech in apps.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DoD agencies confront zero trust challenges, misunderstandings ahead of 2026 deadline
item.description: The Defense Department’s zero trust framework is acting as a catalyst, driving mission owners to industry in search of zero trust solutions The post DoD agencies confront zero trust challenges, misunderstandings ahead of 2026 deadline first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network
